scope.target: Migrate current command/query-driven HTTP boundaries to explicit UseCase/Input with Output/Result/PageResult boundaries and update call sites.
scope.dependency_radius: Application symbols, controllers, DI registration, Wolverine-discovered handlers, and tests.
constraints: Use Rider rename refactoring for programmatic symbol renames where possible.
Align implementation with the approved guideline set.

inputs.architecture_target: Explicit UseCase ports for HTTP entry, message-oriented handlers for bus/consumer/workflow entry, and Result/PageResult for result-pattern outputs.
review_findings: HTTP controllers still use IMessageBus as the default application boundary.
Current command/query symbols are acting as effective use case input/execution units.
user_constraints: Open a workflow record for this implementation migration.
Rename current objects directly and fix call sites.
Use Rider IDE refactoring where possible.

steps: Inventory current symbols and classify HTTP-boundary vs message-oriented usage.
Rename and reshape application symbols by bounded context.
Replace legacy *ResultDto naming used for result-pattern outputs with Result / PageResult aligned models.
Rewrite controllers to use explicit use case injection.
validation: Controllers no longer use IMessageBus as the default application boundary.
Use case naming follows the approved guideline, including Result / PageResult rules.
Affected tests pass or failures are reported explicitly.

results.summary: Implementation workflow opened. Inventory and refactor execution pending.
